evals.prompt: Package this Vite PWA as an Android app using Capacitor. Set up the initial configuration for the Gist Hub app.
evals.expected_output: Created capacitor.config.ts with appId 'com.gisthub.app', appName 'Gist Hub', webDir 'dist', server with androidScheme 'https', and cleartext disabled. Added Capacitor dependencies to package.json. Created Android platform with npx cap add android.
assertions: capacitor.config.ts contains appId 'com.gisthub.app' and appName 'Gist Hub'
webDir is set to 'dist' (Vite's default output directory)
server.androidScheme is set to 'https'
cleartext traffic is disabled
Commands include npm install @capacitor/core @capacitor/cli @capacitor/android

evals.prompt: Our Android build needs to be secure. Configure network security to block cleartext traffic and ensure the PAT is stored safely.
evals.expected_output: Created network_security_config.xml in android/app/src/main/res/xml/ that disables cleartext traffic. AndroidManifest.xml configured with android:usesCleartextTraffic='false'. PAT storage recommendation points to Android Keystore (not SharedPreferences).
assertions: network_security_config.xml is created with cleartextTrafficPermitted='false'
Trust anchors use system certificates only
AndroidManifest.xml references the network security config
android:usesCleartextTraffic='false' is set in AndroidManifest.xml
Output mentions Android Keystore for PAT storage, not SharedPreferences

evals.prompt: Can you set up Capacitor so we can test this on an Android emulator? I want to be able to run the app from the web build.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step instructions: npm run build (Vite), npx cap sync android, npx cap run android (or npx cap open android). Configuration includes webDir pointing to dist/. Includes npx cap doctor for verification.
assertions: Instructions include npm run build before npx cap sync
npx cap sync android command is included
npx cap run android or npx cap open android is included
npx cap doctor command is mentioned for verification
webDir is correctly set to 'dist' for Vite output

evals.prompt: Update the Capacitor config to use a custom splash screen and change the app ID to com.d-o-hub.gist-hub.
evals.expected_output: Updated capacitor.config.ts with new appId 'com.d-o-hub.gist-hub', SplashScreen plugin configuration with launchShowDuration, backgroundColor, and other splash screen settings. Config is valid TypeScript.
assertions: appId is updated to 'com.d-o-hub.gist-hub'
SplashScreen plugin configuration is present under plugins section
SplashScreen includes launchShowDuration and backgroundColor properties
Config is valid TypeScript with proper imports
Existing server and webDir settings are preserved

evals.prompt: What's the minimum Android SDK we should target for maximum device coverage while still supporting modern features?
evals.expected_output: Recommendation for minSdkVersion 22 (Android 5.1) covering ~99% of devices, targetSdkVersion 34, compileSdkVersion 34. Explanation of trade-offs and Capacitor defaults. Note about WebView independence.
assertions: Recommends minSdkVersion 22 (Android 5.1)
Mentions ~99% device coverage claim
Includes targetSdkVersion 34 recommendation
Includes compileSdkVersion 34 recommendation
Notes that Android System WebView updates independently of the app

evals.prompt: I built the web app but the Android app isn't picking up the changes. How do I sync everything?
evals.expected_output: Explanation that npx cap sync android must be run after every web build. Full sequence: npm run build, npx cap sync android, npx cap run android. Explanation of what sync does (copies web assets to android/app/src/main/assets/public/).
assertions: Explains that sync must be run after every web build
Provides the full sequence: build -> sync -> run
Explains that sync copies web assets to the Android project
Mentions the assets go to android/app/src/main/assets/public/
Does not suggest modifying Android Studio project files manually

evals.prompt: Set up Playwright tests to do smoke testing on the Android WebView for our Gist Hub app.
evals.expected_output: Configuration for Playwright Android WebView testing using the playwright-quality skill. Test script that verifies the app loads, navigates to gist list, and basic WebView rendering. Uses Android API from Playwright.
assertions: References the playwright-quality skill for cross-browser testing
Includes Android WebView test configuration
Test verifies app loads and renders basic content
Uses Playwright's Android API (android.launchServer or similar)
References npm run test:android from package.json scripts

evals.prompt: Add OAuth login support to our Android app using Capacitor plugins.
evals.expected_output: This request is out of scope. The response should clarify that OAuth/device flow is a v2 feature per AGENTS.md scope rules, and v1 uses fine-grained PAT authentication. Should NOT implement OAuth. Should redirect to Capacitor Browser plugin if user wants to open GitHub OAuth in a browser instead.
assertions: Response identifies OAuth as a v2 feature per AGENTS.md scope rules
Does NOT implement OAuth login flow
Clarifies that v1 uses PAT authentication only
Mentions Capacitor Browser plugin as an alternative if opening GitHub OAuth in browser
Does not add OAuth-related Capacitor plugins as primary solution
